articles.title: Justin Gaethje Ruining UFC Plans? #ufc
articles.description: Most fans are treating Justin Gaethje beating Ilia Topuria like it would be one of the biggest upsets in UFC history — but Chael Sonnen thinks people are forgetting one important detail: everyone always counts Gaethje out… and he keeps winning anyway. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to comments from Arman Tsarukyan, who pointed out that fans doubted Gaethje against nearly all of his recent opponents. Chael revisits those fights one by one — including matchups against Rafael Fiziev, Max Holloway, and Dustin Poirier. Against Poirier, Gaethje entered a rematch after already losing the first fight… and still finished him with a head kick. Chael explains why a Gaethje victory would completely reshape the UFC lightweight division. Not only would it derail the massive Islam Makhachev vs. Ilia Topuria storyline, it would also force the UFC into an immediate rematch discussion before even considering contenders like Tsarukyan. This isn’t just about an upset — it’s about how one Justin Gaethje win could rewrite the entire future of the sport’s biggest division. For more UFC analysis, Ilia Topuria news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #JustinGaethje #IliaTopuria #IslamMakhachev #ArmanTsarukyan #ChaelSonnen #MMA

articles.title: Unpopular Opinion, What’s New #ufc
articles.description: Everybody is already looking ahead to Alex Pereira vs. Tom Aspinall — but Chael Sonnen believes the MMA world may be overlooking the most dangerous possibility of all: Ciryl Gane pulling off the upset. In this breakdown, Chael explains why respected kickboxers continue warning people about Gane’s advantages in a striking-heavy matchup. According to multiple high-level strikers, Gane is naturally accustomed to heavyweight power, heavyweight kicks, and the kind of impact Pereira has never consistently dealt with before. Chael points out that even veteran kickboxers believe Ciryl Gane would beat Pereira under kickboxing-style conditions — which completely changes how fans should evaluate the fight. Chael also reacts to discussions involving Dustin Poirier and the idea that Justin Gaethje defeating Ilia Topuria would be the biggest upset in UFC history. Chael disagrees. He argues that Gane beating Pereira might not only be possible — it could end up being the bigger shock of the night. While fans obsess over Pereira vs. Aspinall and future heavyweight scenarios, Chael believes Ciryl Gane has quietly become the most dismissed elite heavyweight in the sport. articles.title: If Pereira Wins, Jon Jones Will Be Back
articles.description: What if Alex Pereira beating Ciryl Gane does more than win a championship? What if it brings Jon Jones back? In this video, Chael Sonnen explores a fascinating scenario surrounding the White House card and the future of the heavyweight division. If Alex Pereira defeats Ciryl Gane and UFC CEO Dana White publicly declares him the greatest fighter of all time, Chael believes it could trigger a response from the one man who has owned that conversation for years: Jon Jones. Chael breaks down why legacy, identity, and status matter more to elite competitors than money. Using examples from MMA history, including Anderson Silva's rise in the pound-for-pound rankings, he explains how quickly the GOAT conversation can shift and why Jones may not be willing to watch that happen from the sidelines. The discussion also explores where Tom Aspinall fits into the picture, what a Pereira victory would mean for the heavyweight division, and why the UFC could suddenly find itself with one of the biggest fights in company history. articles.title: Is Eddie Hearn Helping or Hurting Tom Aspinall?
articles.description: Eddie Hearn has inserted himself into the UFC heavyweight title picture—and Chael Sonnen isn't sure whether that's a good thing or a bad thing. In this video, Chael breaks down Eddie Hearn's recent comments about UFC heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall and why they may not be as straightforward as they seem. Hearn has publicly stated that he'll do everything in his power to prevent Aspinall from fighting under his current circumstances, creating questions about the future of the heavyweight division and the winner of Alex Pereira vs. Ciryl Gane. Chael examines whether Hearn is genuinely prepared to keep Aspinall sidelined or if this is simply a negotiation tactic designed to improve Tom's position. He also explores what the UFC's response could be, why relinquishing the title would immediately change the stakes of the heavyweight division, and whether Aspinall himself would ever agree to sit out rather than defend his championship. The bigger question isn't whether Eddie Hearn is right or wrong. It's whether he's helping Tom Aspinall secure a better future—or creating problems that don't need to exist. articles.title: Dana White says Alex Pereira Is the GOAT If…
articles.description: Alex Pereira is one win away from something no fighter has ever accomplished—and Dana White believes it would make him the greatest of all time. In this video, Chael Sonnen breaks down Dana White's stunning comments ahead of the White House card. According to Dana, if Pereira defeats Ciryl Gane and captures the interim UFC heavyweight championship, he would surpass Jon Jones in the GOAT conversation. That's a statement with enormous implications for the future of the sport. Chael explores what a third championship in a third weight class would mean for Pereira's legacy, why the achievement would be historically unique, and how it compares to the accomplishments of legends like Jon Jones, Georges St-Pierre, Anderson Silva, Conor McGregor, and Brock Lesnar. He also examines Pereira's incredible rise from elite kickboxer to one of the most accomplished champions in UFC history. The discussion goes beyond titles and records. It asks a bigger question: what truly makes the greatest fighter of all time? Accomplishments, dominance, revenue, championships, or something else entirely? articles.title: Topuria and Gaethje Got Nasty Fast
articles.description: Justin Gaethje vs Ilia Topuria was already a massive fight. Now it has something every great fight needs: a personal grudge. In this video, Chael Sonnen breaks down the sudden escalation between the UFC stars after weeks of mutual respect. Just days ago, Topuria was publicly praising Gaethje and talking about how much he respected him as both a fighter and a person. Then everything changed. Comments about Topuria's personal life sparked a heated response, turning one of the biggest fights of the year into something much more emotional. Chael explores the psychology behind fight promotion, why some athletes perform best when they feel personally wronged, and whether this rivalry is genuine or simply two elite competitors finding the mindset they need before a championship fight. He also revisits famous examples involving Jon Jones, Sean Strickland, Khamzat Chimaev, and Stipe Miocic to explain why personal conflict can change everything. The White House card already had a title fight, a main event, and two of the sport's biggest stars. Now it has the one thing that always gets fans invested: bad blood. articles.title: Arman Just Bet $1 Million on Justin Gaethje
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan just put his money where his mouth is—and we're not talking about pocket change. After publicly backing Justin Gaethje to defeat Ilia Topuria, Arman reportedly placed a $1 million bet on the outcome. That raises a fascinating question: what does Arman see that the rest of the MMA world doesn't? In this video, Chael Sonnen breaks down Arman's comments, why he believes Gaethje has a real chance, and the overlooked challenges standing in front of the undefeated champion. Chael examines Justin Gaethje's history of proving doubters wrong, from Rafael Fiziev to Dustin Poirier, and explores why some fighters consistently find ways to win when everyone expects them to lose. He also dives into Ilia Topuria's unique circumstances, including a lengthy layoff, relocation from Spain to Miami, training changes, and whether those factors could create problems against a relentless pressure fighter like Gaethje. Most fans see Topuria as the clear favorite. Arman Tsarukyan clearly doesn't. The bigger question is whether he's seeing something hidden in plain sight—or whether he's about to lose a million dollars. Despite being one of the UFC's biggest stars and a former bantamweight champion, O'Malley openly acknowledged that even a dominant win over Aiemann Zahabi won't earn him a title shot. In this video, Chael breaks down why O'Malley finds himself in such a strange position and how the current state of the bantamweight division has created a logjam at the top. Chael examines Merab Dvalishvili's comments about Petr Yan, the delayed title picture at 135 pounds, and why contenders like Cory Sandhagen and Umar Nurmagomedov remain major factors in the division. He also discusses why O'Malley continues to accept difficult fights that many other top fighters would avoid, even when the reward isn't immediately clear. The bigger story isn't whether Sean O'Malley can beat Zahabi. It's what happens afterward. For one of the UFC's biggest draws, the path back to a championship may be harder than ever, even if he keeps winning. articles.title: The UFC Heavyweight Nobody Wants to Wrestle #ufc
articles.description: Chael Sonnen believes Josh Hoekstra may already be one of the most dangerous heavyweight threats in MMA — and he doesn’t think many fighters can even win a round against him. In this breakdown, Chael explains why only a select few names belong in the same conversation as Hoekstra right now. He points to Alex Pereira because of his championship pedigree across multiple combat sports, and gives respect to heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall as one of the division’s true elite. Chael also mentions Gable Steveson as a future possibility, but says the real question surrounding Hoekstra is simple: can he consistently land takedowns against massive heavyweights? Chael breaks down why heavyweight wrestling is completely different from wrestling smaller opponents. If Hoekstra can secure takedowns whenever he wants, then suddenly the entire heavyweight division changes — including potential fights with Aspinall and Pereira. Chael even questions whether fighters like Alexander Volkov could realistically survive rounds against him. Everything may come down to the upcoming matchup with Derrick Lewis. If Hoekstra proves his wrestling translates at the highest level, Chael believes he either becomes UFC champion — or the division avoids him completely. articles.title: Why Ilia Losing Changes EVERYTHING #ufc
articles.description: Some people believe a loss would destroy Ilia Topuria’s momentum forever — but Chael Sonnen completely disagrees. In fact, Chael argues that Ilia may actually have more blockbuster opportunities if he loses than if he wins. Reacting to comments from Justin Gaethje suggesting Ilia might never recover from a defeat, Chael explains why the UFC landscape is filled with massive options for Topuria regardless of the outcome. If Ilia wins, there’s still business with Arman Tsarukyan and endless speculation about a future showdown with Islam Makhachev. But if he loses? Chael believes that opens the door to one of the biggest grudge matches in MMA: Ilia vs. Paddy Pimblett. Chael also compares Topuria’s situation to Alex Pereira moving to light heavyweight after losing at middleweight. According to Chael, fans misremember Pereira’s rise — the loss actually freed him to move divisions and chase a second title. Whether Ilia stays at lightweight, moves divisions, or settles old rivalries, Chael believes his star power survives either way. In today’s UFC, losing one fight doesn’t end a superstar — sometimes it creates an even bigger story.
